name: SignalBrief Social Intel
description:
Real-time social mention intelligence for any brand, competitor, or keyword. Scans Hacker News, Reddit, and GitHub to produce a structured brief with sentiment analysis, theme clustering, urgency-ranked highlights, and actionable recommendations.

Features:
- Scans Hacker News, Reddit, and GitHub simultaneously in real time
- Engagement-weighted sentiment analysis (positive, neutral, negative)
- Theme clustering into six categories: pricing_complaints, support_issues, feature_requests, switching_intent, praise, general_discussion
- Urgency scoring (0-10) combining sentiment signals, churn language, and engagement metrics
- Top most important mentions with direct source links and one-line explanations
- Actionable recommendation based on dominant themes and overall sentiment
- Configurable time windows: 24h, 7d, 30d

Try asking:
- "What are people saying about Stripe on Hacker News, Reddit, and GitHub this week?"
- "List all recent social mentions for Notion over the last 24 hours"
- "Get social briefs for both Vercel and Netlify over the last 7 days to compare their sentiment"
- "Are there any urgent negative mentions about Linear in the last 30 days using the 30d window?"
- "Fetch the 7d social brief for Slack and draft an email summarizing any switching intent signals"
- "Check for any mentions of the obscure unreleased feature Xyz123 over 30d to see if it leaked"
- "Give me a full multi-platform social intelligence brief for OpenAI, analyze the top urgency-ranked mentions, and suggest a strategic product pivot"

Agent tips:
- Use the 7d window (default) for a balanced view; 24h for breaking issues; 30d for trend analysis
- The tool returns both human-readable text (summary + recommendation) and full structured data via structuredContent
- Theme categories map directly to actionable business responses
- Rate limited to 10 requests/minute due to upstream API constraints
- CRITICAL: Do NOT execute internal retry loops to self-heal. If the tool returns data (even if limited to one platform) or if searchExhausted is true, accept the data immediately as the final complete answer and return it to the user. Do not re-query with altered parameters to save strict latency.
- CRITICAL: If the target brand name is a common dictionary word (e.g., Slack, Linear, Notion), you MUST automatically append contextual keywords like "software" or "app" to the 'q' parameter on your very first attempt to prevent semantic noise (e.g., use "Slack software" instead of "Slack").
